content.help: What happens when you say a spell/power/shout name with no prefix. Equip: puts it in your hand. Cast: fires it instantly. Saying 'cast' or 'equip' first always overrides this.
Which hand 'equip <spell>' uses (and a bare name when Default action is Equip). Saying 'equip left/right/dual' always overrides this.
ON: 'cast <spell>' fires the spell instantly (costs magicka). OFF: 'cast <spell>' equips it so you cast it yourself.
Concentration spells (e.g. Flames) misbehave when instant-cast, so they are equipped instead by default. Turn ON to instant-cast them anyway.
Long-charge spells (e.g. Mass Paralysis) are equipped instead of instant-cast by default. Turn ON to instant-cast them anyway.
A spell counts as 'long-charge' when its charge time is at or above this many seconds. Affects the rule above.

OFF (default): SILENT - you spoke the words, so you are the voice; the shout fires its effect with no character vocalization. ON: your character vocalizes the full Thu'um for every shout. Movement shouts (Whirlwind Sprint, Become Ethereal) always vocalize either way.

ON (default): script/movement shouts (Whirlwind Sprint, Become Ethereal) use the real shout pipeline so their motion fires; simple shouts stay instant. OFF: force fast instant-cast for ALL shouts. Try OFF if shouts misbehave in exclusive-fullscreen.
ON (default): voice-casting a shout puts back whatever shout/power you had equipped afterwards (the mod equips the spoken shout to fire it). OFF: the voice-cast shout stays equipped.

ON (default): fires shouts at the word you spoke almost instantly instead of a 0.2-1.2s charge-up - movement shouts (Whirlwind Sprint) and all effects still fire. Briefly retimes the game's shout thresholds around a one-frame key tap. Turn OFF only if shouts fire the wrong word level on your setup (legacy timed-hold fallback).
EXPERIMENTAL. Plays the visible shout body animation on the instant-cast path. OFF by default; turn ON only if you want the animation and accept it may look rough.
How closely what you say must match a spell name to count. Lower = more sensitive/forgiving but more wrong casts; higher = stricter, may ignore valid speech. Default 0.62. Applies immediately.

ON (default): nudges the recognizer toward your actual spell/shout names, which helps proper nouns and dragon words. Takes effect after 'Restart recognizer' (Developer page). If it starts hearing spells when you didn't say one, lower the strength below or turn this off.

How hard to bias toward your spell names (only matters when biasing is ON). Higher = more likely to hear the right spell, but also more likely to 'hear' one in noise. Default 1.5. Applies after Restart recognizer.

How long a pause after you stop speaking before a command is sent. The main responsiveness knob. Lower = snappier but may cut off multi-word names; higher = waits longer, safer for long names. Default 0.45 s. Takes effect after Restart recognizer (Developer page) or relaunch.
A firm backstop: end the utterance after this much continuous silence even mid-phrase. Usually leave at default. Default 0.70 s. Takes effect after Restart recognizer or relaunch.
Safety cap: force the utterance to end after this many seconds so recording can't run away. Most players never need to change this. Default 20 s. Takes effect after Restart recognizer or relaunch.

content.text: Limit huge spell lists to favorites
content.help: OFF (default): every known spell is voice-castable. ON: once you know 600+ spells, only FAVORITED and equipped spells (plus all powers/shouts) are listened for, which improves speed and accuracy.

content.text: If commands aren't heard: check your mic, then use Restart recognizer (Developer)

content.help: ON: HOLD the push-to-talk key to listen, release to send. OFF (default): always listening unless you mute with the listen-toggle key.
HOLD this key to listen; release to send. Click and press the key to bind.
Optional modifier held with the push-to-talk key (e.g. Alt for Alt+T). Leave unbound for none.
ON: PRESS the toggle key to switch hands-free listening on/off (shows 'Speak Up: On/Off'). Handy for muting during conversations.
PRESS to toggle listening on/off (hands-free). Click and press the key to bind.
Optional modifier held with the toggle key (e.g. Alt for Alt+T). Tip: set push-to-talk key = T and toggle key = T with this modifier = Alt -> hold T to talk, Alt+T to toggle.
ON: logs every recognized phrase + diagnostics to SpeakUp.log (useful for bug reports). OFF: only warnings/errors. Defaults ON for first-run setup; turn OFF for normal play.
When ON, writes the full live phrase-to-command list to SpeakUp.log once. Useful for diagnosing why a name isn't recognized or conflicts with another. Defaults ON for first-run setup.

Flip this to re-launch the speech recognizer WITHOUT restarting the game - use it if the mic was busy/disconnected/muted at launch (after you fix it), or to apply changed Responsiveness sliders. Closing the MCM applies it; the mic re-opens.
